preamble: The voiced alveolar plosive [d] shifts to the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] through yod-coalescence, a well-documented palatalization process in English where /d/ coalesces with a following /j/ to form the affricate /dʒ/, which can further reduce to [ʃ] in rapid or casual speech contexts.

examples.note: Yod-coalescence in rapid speech where /dj/ coalesces to /dʒ/, and in casual registers may further reduce toward [ʃ]
Historical evolution from Old French /sjon/ → English /ʃən/ (as in "mission", "passion")
Ongoing allophonic process in Modern English before high front vowels and palatal approximants

period: Old English to Modern English (historical); ongoing in contemporary speech
